methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series B → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.9x) to $40M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 5 same-sector + same-stage peers in AI & Software. Range: $500M - $32.0B.

data.narrative:
MakinaRocks is a Series B-stage AI & Software company that has raised $40M to date. Valuation analysis draws from a comp universe of 5 same-stage, same-sector private peers—Safe Superintelligence ($32.0B), Physical Intelligence ($5.6B), World Labs ($5.0B), Nominal ($1.0B), and Photoroom ($500M)—alongside one public comparable (PLTR).

Two triangulation methods were applied. Stage-progression analysis uses the $40M total raised as a proxy base value and applies a 1.9x step-up multiplier typical for Series B advancement, implying a valuation of $76M. Peer-median analysis anchors to the 5-company median valuation of $5.0B, with a range spanning $500M to $32.0B. The disparity reflects substantial variance in AI & Software valuations at this stage, driven by differences in model architecture, capital efficiency, and commercialization progress.

Triangulation across both methods yields a valuation range of $65M (low) to $5.8B (high), with a median of $2.5B. The wide band reflects the current heterogeneity of Series B AI valuations. Material uncertainties include revenue traction (not provided), technical differentiation relative to peers, and burn rate relative to raised capital. The stage-progression method produces a significantly lower bound than peer comparison, suggesting either that MakinaRocks' funding history trails typical Series B entrants or that peer comps skew toward exceptionally well-capitalized or proven cohorts.
